figures.caption: Fig. 7–1.Two sets of orthogonal curves which can represent equipotentials in a two-dimensional electrostatic field.
Fig. 7–2.The field near the point CC is the same as that in Fig. 7–1.
Fig. 7–3.The field in a quadrupole lens.
Fig. 7–4.Curves of constant U(x,y)U(x,y) and V(x,y)V(x,y) from Eq. (7.12).
Fig. 7–5.The electric field near the edge of a thin grounded plate.
Fig. 7–6.Motion in a plasma wave. The electrons at the plane aa move to a′a', and those at bb move to b′b'.
Fig. 7–7.The variation of the potential near the surface of a colloidal particle. DD is the Debye length.
Fig. 7–8.Equipotential surfaces above a uniform grid of charged wires.
